name: Santuario del Saliente — circular from Albox — Valle del Almanzora
name_original: Santuario del Saliente — circular desde Albox — Valle del Almanzora
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Albox traverse a diverse landscape, situated within the Almanzora Valley between the Sierra de los Filabres and Sierra de las Estancias mountain ranges. The region features arid hills dotted with olive, citrus, and almond groves, contrasting with the green river basin of the Almanzora. Cyclists encounter dramatic hills and valleys, offering varied terrain and rewarding views across steppe-like environments. Best touring cycling routes around Albox The most popular touring cycling route is Santuario del Saliente — circular from Albox — Valle del Almanzora, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers scenic views around the historic Santuario del Saliente. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santuario del Saliente – Albox loop from Albox, a difficult 26.5 miles (42.6 km) path. This route presents challenging terrain with significant ascent, providing expansive views of the surrounding mountain ranges. Local touring cyclists also love the Almanzora – Cantoria Tunnel loop from Albox, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through the Almanzora Valley, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. This moderate route incorporates a section of the Vía Verde Valle del Almanzora, featuring the historic Cantoria Tunnel. Touring cycling around Albox is defined by arid hills, fertile river basins, and mountainous terrain, with options ranging from moderate rides to challenging excursions. The network offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from those seeking a relaxed pace to experienced cyclists. The routes in Albox are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albox's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Albox?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Albox, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes traverse varied landscapes, from arid hills to fertile river basins and mountainous terrain.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling in Albox?
A: The touring cycling routes around Albox primarily range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 16 difficult routes, often featuring significant elevation changes due to the region's mountainous geography between the Sierra de los Filabres and Sierra de las Estancias.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Albox area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are popular among touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055822448/santuario-del-saliente-circular-from-albox-valle-del-almanzora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente — circular from Albox — Valle del Almanzora&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.6 km loop offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora – Cantoria Tunnel loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 26.4 km ride that includes a section of the Vía Verde Valle del Almanzora.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Albox?
A: Albox offers a striking contrast of landscapes. You'll cycle through arid hills dotted with olive, citrus, and almond groves, experience dramatic valleys, and encounter steppe-like environments. The routes also touch upon the greener Almanzora River basin, with its lemon and almond trees, providing a diverse visual experience.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can visit along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant points of interest. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century pilgrimage center offering magnificent views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Vía Verde Valle del Almanzora, is another highlight. Other points of interest include the Hermitage of San Antonio and Viewpoint of Santa Cruz for panoramic views, and the Pottery-Museum Los Puntas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Albox?
A: The touring cycling routes in Albox are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the rewarding views of the surrounding mountain ranges and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde Valle del Almanzora Natural Trail is a spectacular route that follows an old railway line, part of the Guadix-Almendricos Greenway. This trail offers a unique perspective on the region's iron ore culture and features railway constructions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing diverse landscapes from fertile meadows to steppe environments.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Albox?
A: While the guide doesn't specify, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for touring cycling in Albox. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making the significant elevation changes and longer routes more enjoyable than the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a more challenging touring cycling experience?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Albox are classified as difficult, offering significant ascents and demanding terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albox – Santuario del Saliente loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt; covers over 50 km with more than 800m of elevation gain, providing a robust challenge for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the mountain ranges around Albox?
A: Yes, the region is situated between the Sierra de los Filabres and Sierra de las Estancias mountain ranges, and many routes incorporate these areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente – Albox loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers expansive views of these surrounding mountain ranges and presents challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes available from Albox?
A: For those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arboleas Station – Cantoria Tunnel loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option, spanning over 75 km with more than 1200m of elevation gain. This route allows for an extended exploration of the Almanzora Valley and its historical railway infrastructure.
tips: Albox, with approximately 11,000 inhabitants, is located at the foot of the Sierra de las Estancias, a mountain range known for its impressive natural landscapes and popular hiking trails.

Built during the last decades of the 18th century, the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora del Buen Retiro de los Desamparados del Saliente is one of the most important pilgrimage centers in southeastern Spain. It is located in the municipality of Albox. It houses the well-known Virgen del Saliente,

Sanctuary of Our Lady of Saliente
